statement.en: Biological age is a concept, not a construct : assessments of healthy aging using epigenetic clocks, physiological, and functional measures of aging
statement.summary: The full abstract for this thesis is available in the body of the thesis, and will be available when the embargo expires.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The thesis title presents a conceptual claim about biological age ontology (concept vs. construct distinction) that remains under embargo. The claim is substantive and contestable—whether biological age is a 'natural concept' (mind-independent category) vs. a 'construct' (observer-dependent framework) is actively debated in gerontology and philosophy of science. Without access to the full argument and evidence, the truth-value is provisional and subject to revision upon publication. The date (2026) indicates this is a preprint/dissertation in validation phase.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 時間は数字ではなく、細胞が刻む多次元の足跡である。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 時の層が重なり、外と内と動きが一つのリズムを奏でる。
